what_it_does: Defines a React Registry canvas that presents workshop capabilities as workstations with searchable shelves of registry types, tile thumbnails, inspector opening, quick-add flows, a Vi rail, and an advanced builder entry point.
observations: Workstations are static data describing decks, brochures, widgets, wizards, and shared materials.
Registry state tracks search text, collapsed workstation state, inspected type id, and quick-adder context.
Registry queries window.CV_REGISTRY for types and listens for registry changes through subscribe callbacks.
Tiles render TypeThumb thumbnails when window.TypeThumb is available.
The page delegates inspection, Vi interaction, quick adding, and advanced authoring to TypeInspector, ViStudioRail, QuickAdder, and onOpenBuilder.
standouts: The registry is organized around user-facing production categories rather than raw registry layers.
Each shelf combines query metadata with add metadata for creating related types.
The UI keeps thumbnails central and reveals type name/provenance through a tile overlay.
conventions: Uses global React aliases with file-specific suffixes such as useState_reg and useMemo_reg.
Uses global runtime objects and components through window and unimported symbols.
Uses data-driven rendering from WORKSTATIONS into Workstation, Shelf, and ShelfTile components.
Uses CSS class prefixes centered on rs- plus provenance classes.
concerns: RegistryFloor assigns window.CV_REGISTRY to R but does not use it.
Several dependencies are implicit globals, so this file relies on runtime ordering outside the module system.
The direct thumbnail editing idea in the header is not implemented inside this file; tiles open inspection instead.
notes: This file is the registry floor UI that maps registry type data into workstation shelves with inspect and add actions.
questions: What exact contract does CV_REGISTRY.subscribe return for cleanup?
What shape does QuickAdder expect when opened from the floating add button with an empty adder object?
Where are TypeInspector, QuickAdder, ViStudioRail, ViShape, CvIcon, and TypeThumb defined?
purpose_vs_actual: The workstation-based organization matches the stated purpose; the file implements browsing, searching, inspecting, and add entry points, while direct thumbnail editing is only suggested by comments rather than implemented here.
apparent_themes: Progressive disclosure through inspector and overlays
intention_signals: It appears to be trying to make the registry feel like a workshop floor where users choose what they want to make, inspect visual type examples, and add new materials without starting from an abstract database schema.
novelty: The static workstation ledger blends user-language categories with registry layer queries and creation metadata in the same structure.
contribution: Contributes a capability-oriented registry browsing surface, workstation and shelf taxonomy, visual tile presentation, registry subscription wiring, and add/inspect entry points that a fused system could retain as one interaction model for navigating code/design substrates.
summary_for_embedding: A React Registry canvas that renders workshop capability workstations, searchable registry shelves, thumbnail type tiles, inspector state, quick-add flows, Vi rail context, and advanced builder access.
intention_for_embedding: It was for reframing a type registry as a visual workshop floor organized by what users can create rather than by abstract substrate layers.

symbols.does: Renders the full registry shell and coordinates search, collapse, inspection, quick-add, Vi rail, and advanced-builder state.
symbols.description: Registry is the top-level canvas component. It subscribes to CV_REGISTRY changes, computes per-workstation user/Vi addition counts, renders the floor, conditionally shows the type inspector and quick adder, and keeps the Vi rail synchronized with the currently inspected type.

symbols.does: Sets the currently inspected type id.
symbols.description: openInspector is a small state helper used as the common entry point for opening a type in the inspector. It stores the provided id in inspected state.

symbols.does: Clears the currently inspected type id.
symbols.description: closeInspector is the paired state helper for closing the inspector. It resets inspected to null, which removes the TypeInspector from the render tree.

symbols.does: Renders the registry header, search field, advanced button, and workstation list.
symbols.description: RegistryFloor is the main scrollable floor view. It receives state and callbacks from Registry, shows explanatory header copy, exposes search clearing and advanced authoring actions, and maps WORKSTATIONS into Workstation components.

symbols.does: Renders one collapsible workstation section with shelves and an additions count.
symbols.description: Workstation presents a single production category such as slide decks or widgets. It toggles collapsed state from its header, displays custom addition counts when present, and renders each shelf with the correct add callback.

symbols.does: Queries registry types for one shelf and renders matching type tiles plus an add button.
symbols.description: Shelf subscribes to registry changes, queries CV_REGISTRY using the shelf query merged with the current search string, optionally filters by an extending type, and displays the resulting count and horizontal tile track.

symbols.does: Renders a clickable thumbnail tile for a registry type.
symbols.description: ShelfTile wraps a TypeThumb preview in a button. It applies provenance styling, shows name and non-built-in provenance in an overlay, and calls onOpen when selected.

symbols.does: Renders the persistent floating add action.
symbols.description: FloatingAddButton is a compact button with a ViShape icon and Add label. It opens the generic quick-adder flow through its onClick callback.
